meta.description: Complete DSSM case study database — all regions from all papers encoded

egypt.name: Egypt / Nile Valley

qurta_petroglyphs.significance: Earliest Nile Valley symbolic evidence — figurative animal petroglyphs

wadi_kubbaniya.significance: Plant processing, ochre use — proto-symbolic behaviors

badarian.significance: First standardized ceramic tradition on Nile — Stage 2 groundwork

naqada_i_ii.significance: Full symbolic saturation — painted pottery, prestige goods, burial differentiation, predynastic expansion

dynasty_i.significance: Writing preserves already-stable symbolic system — not created to manage new complexity

old_kingdom.significance: Full monumentality — Great Pyramid as Stage 4 material expression

four_stabilization_criteria: Landscape anchoring through Nile flood cycle (reliable annual ritual rhythm)
Portable ochre/ceramic symbolic program before monumentality
Settlement nucleation along predictable flood corridor
Writing as preservative cap on already-saturated system

negative_comparison_cases.sub_saharan_africa: Similar symbolic precursors but no axial river constraint → forager-mnemonic pathway persists
negative_comparison_cases.australia: Oldest continuous symbolic tradition but no P7 threshold crossed → Stage 2 indefinitely
negative_comparison_cases.late_neolithic_europe: Megalithic monuments without full Stage 2 saturation → monuments not diagnostic of Stage 3

mesopotamia.name: Mesopotamia / Tigris-Euphrates

shanidar_cave.significance: Burial with flowers — disputed symbolic evidence; cannot confirm Stage 1 under DSSM

mureybit.significance: Early village, wild grain storage — proto-agricultural symbolic rhythms

hassuna_samarra.significance: Painted pottery with geometric motifs; evidence of symbolic transmission

halaf.significance: Flower petal arithmetic (4, 8, 16, 32, 64) demonstrates systematic mathematical cognition before writing. Standardized production across 400+ sites.

ubaid.significance: First monumental temples, long-distance exchange networks, social stratification

uruk.significance: Full urban civilization — world's first true city (~80,000 population at peak)

uruk_writing.significance: Administrative complexity of world's first urban system overwhelms oral/token management → cuneiform emerges

mesopotamia.key_insight: Clay tokens (proto-accounting) predate cuneiform by ~2000 years — Stage 3 structural solution that eventually failed as urban complexity grew, forcing Stage 4 writing

east_asia.name: East Asia (China / Yellow River / Yangtze)

yangshao.significance: Painted ceramics with standardized geometric programs

hongshan.significance: Jade coiled dragons, pig-dragon pendants = portable symbolic system at Stage 3 WITHOUT monuments. This is the diagnostic marker of the Portable-Craft pathway.

liangzhu.significance: Cong tubes, bi discs — advanced jade symbolic grammar

erlitou.significance: First Chinese urban monuments — monumentality arrives LATE as compression of already-saturated jade-based symbolic system

shang_oracle_bones.significance: Writing arrives much later than other regions — portable-craft pathway delayed Stage 4 transition

east_asia.pathway_diagnostic: Jade as portable symbolic anchor precedes monumentality by 2,000–3,000 years. This is unique to East Asia and defines the Portable-Craft pathway.

mesoamerica.name: Mesoamerica (Olmec / Maya / Aztec)

pre_olmec.significance: Ceramic traditions, early figurines, incipient ritual centers

olmec_formative.key_medium: Jade — portable symbolic anchors (same diagnostic as East Asia)
olmec_formative.significance: Colossal heads, jade masks, long-distance jade exchange network. No axial river. Symbolic system transmitted through inter-site ritual networks.

preclassic_maya.significance: Pyramid temples, ballcourt complexes, stela program begins

classic_maya.significance: Late Stage 4 consolidation — writing as prestige/historical preservation system, not administrative management

mesoamerica.pathway_diagnostic: No axial river required. Symbolic system transmitted through inter-site exchange networks (jade, obsidian, cacao). Ritual networks are the transmission vector.

indus_valley.name: Indus Valley / Harappan Civilization

mehrgarh_ia.significance: Earliest South Asian settlement — mud-brick architecture, proto-agriculture

mehrgarh_ib.significance: Figurines, standardized ornaments — first DSSM-compliant symbolic system in South Asia

early_harappan.significance: Standardized brick sizes, weight systems, proto-Indus signs appearing

mature_harappan.significance: Full quotidian symbolic saturation — standardized weights across 1.5 million km², stamp seals with unicorn motif, planned cities with identical grid layouts. No palace, no temple, no writing system in phonetic sense.

indus_script.writing_outcome: Structural resolution — seals encode collective identity, not phonetics
indus_script.significance: Indus script is NOT a phonetic writing system. It is a structural resolution to the coordination problem — collective symbolic codes that made phonetic writing unnecessary.

indus_valley.pathway_diagnostic: Symbolic system embedded in infrastructure: standardized bricks, weights, seals, drainage systems. No monuments needed because the entire built environment IS the symbolic statement. Mercantile pathway.

sri_lanka.name: Sri Lanka (Balangoda Culture / Wanniyala-Aetto)

batadomba_lena.significance: Ochre use, geometric marks — early Stage 2 evidence

fa_hien_lena.significance: Microlithic tools, ornaments — full Stage 2 groundwork

peak_saturation.cv_metric: CV = 4.7% across 20,000 years of microlithic production
peak_saturation.significance: Full symbolic saturation by Stage 2 metrics — no Stage 3 transition required

current.note: Wanniyala-Aetto (Veddas) maintain continuous symbolic traditions without monumentality or writing — 100,000+ year continuity
current.significance: CONTROL CASE — proves that Stage 3 and Stage 4 are NOT inevitable outcomes of symbolic development

sri_lanka.key_insight: Sri Lanka demonstrates 'symbolic sufficiency' — a Stage 2 system can be stable, sophisticated, and continuous without ever requiring material amplification. This is not failure but a legitimate alternative pathway.
sri_lanka.cv_data: Microlithic CV = 4.7% over 20,000 years — extraordinary standardization without writing or monuments
sri_lanka.pathway_diagnostic: Forager communities maintain symbolic stability through oral/embodied transmission. P7 threshold never crossed because population density never required it.

peak_vinca.significance: Europe's first DSSM-saturated symbolic system — 700+ standardized symbols across 1,000 km territory

vinca_collapse.significance: Structural disruption (climate, population pressure) leads to symbolic system collapse — not absorbed into higher civilization

vinca_symbols.distribution: Across 1,000+ km from Serbia to Hungary
vinca_symbols.link_to_indus: Rectangular seal design concept appears in both Vinča and Indus — possible transmission via Central Asian networks, or convergent solution
vinca_symbols.script_status: NOT writing — distributed symbolic system. 'Saturation index' = cross-media redundancy + entropy + enforcement nodes

saturation_index.formula: Saturation Index = Cross-Media Redundancy × Entropy⁻¹ × Enforcement Node Density
saturation_index.description: Vinča scored high on redundancy and enforcement but collapsed before Stage 4 integration

mehrgarh.name: Mehrgarh (South Asia, pre-Harappan)

period_i.significance: South Asia's earliest DSSM-compliant system. Mud-brick architecture, aceramic phase, proto-symbolic artifacts.

period_ii_iii.significance: Figurines, ornaments, standardized production — full Stage 2 groundwork for Harappan trajectory

mehrgarh.relationship_to_harappan: Mehrgarh provides the ~4,000-year Stage 1-2 groundwork before Harappan Stage 3. Continuous thread from 7000 BCE to Harappan peak at 2600 BCE.

O5_architecture.note: Constructed stalagmite ring structures — clear non-domestic construction

O6_standardization.note: Consistent construction technique across ring structures

bruniquel_cave.interpretation: Bruniquel demonstrates proto-symbolic spatial organization but the absence of portable symbolic anchors (O4=0) confirms the EWMH limit. Neanderthals could externalize symbolic behavior into fixed space but not into portable transmissible objects.
bruniquel_cave.ewmh_relevance: The 0 score on O4 is not a data gap — it is a species-level cognitive constraint. Neanderthals invested in spatial symbolism (ring construction requiring planning and cooperation) but could not project symbolic content into portable media.

omo_kibish.interpretation: Earliest anatomically modern Homo sapiens, but behavioral modernity has not yet emerged. Only basic pigment use — insufficient for even Stage 1 classification. Confirms that anatomical modernity precedes behavioral modernity by ~100,000 years.

blombos_cave.location: Blombos Cave, Western Cape, South Africa

O1_pigment.note: Ochre processing kits — systematic, multi-step pigment production

O2_ornaments.note: Nassarius shell beads — perforated, worn, standardized

O3_engravings.note: Engraved geometric patterns on ochre — systematic hashtag/crosshatch design

O4_portable_anchors.note: Complete portable symbolic system — beads function as social information storage

O5_architecture.note: Cave use structured but not constructed

O6_standardization.note: Bead production CV extremely low — consistent perforation technique

blombos_cave.interpretation: Perfect 12/12 score. Blombos is the diagnostic benchmark for full behavioral modernity. The presence of a complete portable symbolic system (ochre processing + beads + engravings + standardized production) confirms Stage 2 DSSM classification at 100,000–70,000 BCE — 70,000 years before the European Upper Paleolithic 'revolution'.
blombos_cave.ewmh_relevance: Blombos proves that enhanced working memory enabling portable symbolic systems was present in African Homo sapiens by at least 100,000 BCE — not 40,000 BCE as the Eurocentric model assumed.

sulawesi.location: Sulawesi Cave Art sites, Maros-Pangkep karst, Indonesia

O1_pigment.note: Red ochre and mulberry pigment — deliberate color selection

O2_ornaments.note: No portable ornament evidence in cave context

O3_engravings.note: Figurative art — warty pig and banteng cattle

O4_portable_anchors.note: Possible portable art but not confirmed in assemblage

O5_architecture.note: Cave as structured symbolic space — deliberate site selection and organization

O6_standardization.note: Layered stencils 35,000 years apart — implies transmission but sample limited

sulawesi.key_finding: Oldest known symbolic system in Island Southeast Asia. Layered hand stencils 35,000 years apart in same location demonstrates symbolic transmission across 1,400 generations — Stage 2 hallmark.
sulawesi.interpretation: Sulawesi forces a global reassessment of symbolic origins. Figurative art as old as Europe's Chauvet cave appears in Southeast Asia — consistent with African origin model and rapid dispersal of behavioral modernity.

halafian_pottery.location: Halaf Culture sites — Syria, Iraq, Turkey

O3_engravings.note: Geometric pottery motifs with arithmetic structure

halafian_pottery.key_finding: Flower petal count arithmetic: designs consistently use 4, 8, 16, 32, 64 — demonstrating mathematical cognition embedded in ceramic production 3,000 years before writing. This is cognitive offloading into pottery before Mesopotamian cuneiform.
halafian_pottery.cv_metric: CV = 0.12 across 400+ Halaf sites spanning 400,000 km² — demonstrates symbolic transmission network of extraordinary coherence
